For everyone predicting she’s going to end up with Chris, I highly recommend you look into Celine song’s breakout film, past lives. That movie wrecked me. Celine doesn’t tend to do the common romcom ending, but her films are always a commentary on real life relations and identity. I wouldn’t be surprised if Dakota ends up with Pedro just because she seems to believe that love is materialism, but she also may just go her own way. I don’t think she’ll end up with the “poor” person because that’s fantasy, not realism.
I want to believe that is going to be realistic and zero stereotypes🕯🕯🕯
I really hope this film is a spiritual successor of Past Lives.

I wouldn’t say “love is materialism” but having agency in terms of financials can go a long way in a relationship. Chris’s character kind of reminds me in my last relationship. He doesn’t seem to have his shit fully together and looks to be struggling to make ends meet. Being in that situation while dating someone is fucking hard. There’s so many things you want to do for them but you don’t have the financial means to do so. Eventually, fights over money will happen, it’s just natural. It then bleeds into other aspects of the relationship… Then you start to slowly resent each other. Then you break up.

Then they end up dating someone that does have their shit together… and then they get engaged by the time you finally get your shit together…

Yeah this movie is gonna really hit me close to home.

@kevorobo this is a good breakdown! I agree, Celine rarely paints her characters as shallow so I look forward to seeing the reveal on what materialists means for love. The trailer makes it seem like it’s about money but maybe it also means something deeper than surface level, like spiritual, not just material. So many levels. 😮😮😮

Now that I’m typing this out, I hope to see a deeper than material/spiritual connection between Dakota and Chris’s characters, but to see that it’s the physical that we also have to pay attention to in this reality, so material security also does matter.

If that’s one of the conversations Celine‘s going after, I’m here for it.
